senses.definition: the act of not giving attention to something before it becomes a problem or necessary to address
examples: The doctor warned that prenatal care should not go on with preneglect, as it can affect the baby's health.
Preneglect of early warning signs can lead to more serious health issues later.

senses.definition: pertaining to or showing neglect before it is needed or before something occurs
examples: Preneglectful planning failed to address potential risks.
The prenatal care was criticized for being preneglectful of certain warning signs.

senses.definition: a failure to give proper attention or care beforehand; preemptive negligence
examples: The report cited prenegligence as a factor in the accident.
Prenegligence can complicate liability assessments in some cases.

senses.definition: negligent in a preemptive or forethoughtful manner; failing to take precautions in advance
examples: Experts warned that prenegligent behavior could lead to accidents.
The policy was criticized as prenegligent in anticipating hazards.

senses.definition: to negotiate something before a formal negotiation begins
examples: The team decided to prenegotiate the terms to speed up the deal.
They prenegotiate several details to avoid later disputes.

senses.definition: having negotiated something before the formal negotiation began
examples: They prenegotiated the framework and moved to a quick signing.
A prenegotiated draft reduced meeting time.

senses.definition: negotiates something before formal negotiations start
examples: The department prenegotiates the budget items.
They prenegotiates minor points to streamline the process.

senses.definition: the act of negotiating something before formal talks
examples: Prenegotiating can help avoid stalemates during the official talks.
Prenegotiating several clauses saved time.

senses.definition: the act or process of negotiating before the main negotiation
examples: The prenegotiation stage set the framework for the talks.
Prenegotiation helped outline acceptable terms.

senses.definition: multiple negotiations that occur before the main negotiation
examples: There were several prenegotiations before the final agreement.
Prenegotiations laid the groundwork for the treaty.

senses.definition: relating to the period before the use of stone tools in a given culture; prehistoric
examples: The cave contained records from the preneolithic era.
Pren eolithic artifacts were scarce and rudimentary.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling a nephritic or kidney-related condition before onset
examples: Prenep hritic symptoms are not commonly described in modern medicine.
The term appears in historical medical texts as pren ephritic.

senses.definition: before neural development or relating to the pre-nervous system stage
examples: Preneural development is studied in embryology.
Preneural research focuses on early neural formation.

senses.definition: relating to or causing neuralgic pain before onset; pre-nerve pain symptoms
examples: Preneuralgic discomfort can precede a migraine for some people.
The term is rarely used outside clinical descriptions.

senses.definition: the period immediately before night; late evening
examples: They planned outdoor activities for prenight light.
Prenight there was a chill breeze.

senses.definition: not noble or lacking noble qualities; unworthy of nobility
examples: The court dismissed the prenoble actions as unbecoming.
His prenoble behavior offended many nobles.

senses.definition: occurring before a node or junction; before a branching point in biology or networks
examples: Prenodal analysis helped map the early pathway.
The prenodal region showed preliminary connections.

senses.definition: a given name or first name, especially a name used before others; a personal name
examples: In some cultures, the prenomen is followed by a family name.
The prenomen appears on formal documents.

senses.definition: plural of prenomen; multiple given names
examples: The descendants carried prenomens from both sides of the family.
She has several prenomens recorded in the registry.

senses.definition: a list of given names; sometimes used in scholastic or formal contexts
examples: The prenomina list was used during the ceremony.
prenomina appears in some Latin-derived academic terms.

senses.definition: relating to or placed before a noun (as an adjective in grammar)
examples: A prenominal modifier appears before the noun it describes.
In many languages, prenominal articles mark definiteness.

senses.definition: to nominate someone before a formal nomination process; to pre-nominate
examples: They prenominate a candidate to streamline the committee selection.
Prenominate the teams to avoid delays in voting.

senses.definition: nominated beforehand; pre-nominated
examples: The prenominated candidate faced questions at the meeting.
A prenominated list was circulated in advance.

senses.definition: nominates someone beforehand; pre-nominates
examples: The committee prenominates several potential leaders.
They prenominates individuals for future roles.

senses.definition: the act of nominating someone beforehand; pre-nominating
examples: Prenominating can speed up the official process.
Prenominating several candidates reduces uncertainty.
